examTitle: Education & Learning: CEFR B2 Mock Reading & Use of English

parts.partTitle: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze on Educational Technology
parts.instructions: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
parts.content: The new educational technology platform has been widely adopted in schools around the world. The company behind the platform has announced a (1) _______ (LAUNCH) in their latest product, which includes a range of interactive tools and resources for both teachers and students. According to the company, the platform has been designed to (2) _______ (SUPPORT) teachers in their classrooms, providing them with real-time feedback and assessment tools. The platform has already been (3) _______ (USE) by over a million teachers and educators worldwide, with many reporting significant improvements in student engagement and outcomes. The company has also announced a (4) _______ (PARTNERSHIP) with a leading educational publisher, which will provide teachers with access to a range of high-quality educational resources.

questions.question: Gap 1
options: A. Introduction
B. Launch
C. Development
D. Implementation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'launch' because it refers to the act of introducing a new product or service to the market. The other options are incorrect because 'introduction' is a more general term, 'development' refers to the process of creating something new, and 'implementation' refers to the process of putting something into practice.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 2
options: A. Enhance
B. Support
C. Develop
D. Improve
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'support' because it refers to the act of providing help or assistance to teachers in their classrooms. The other options are incorrect because 'enhance' refers to making something better or more effective, 'develop' refers to the process of creating something new, and 'improve' refers to making something better or more effective.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 3
options: A. Adopt
B. Use
C. Implement
D. Develop
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'use' because it refers to the act of employing or utilizing something. The other options are incorrect because 'adopt' refers to the act of accepting or embracing something, 'implement' refers to the process of putting something into practice, and 'develop' refers to the process of creating something new.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 4
options: A. Contract
B. Partnership
C. Agreement
D. Collaboration
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'partnership' because it refers to a business or professional relationship between two or more organizations. The other options are incorrect because 'contract' refers to a written agreement between two or more parties, 'agreement' refers to a general understanding or arrangement, and 'collaboration' refers to the act of working together with others.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 2: Open Cloze on Educational Psychology
parts.instructions: Read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
parts.content: Educational psychologists have long been interested in the way that students learn and develop. Research has shown that students who are (1) _______ (ENGAGED) in their learning tend to perform better than those who are not. One reason for this is that engaged students are more likely to take an active role in their own learning, rather than simply passively receiving information. This can help to (2) _______ (DEVELOP) their critical thinking skills, as well as their ability to work independently.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'engaged' because it refers to the state of being actively interested or involved in something. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing an open cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'develop' because it refers to the process of growing or becoming more complex. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing an open cloze task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 3: Word Formation on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
parts.content: The increasing (1) _______ (POPULATION) of the world's cities has led to a number of environmental problems. One of the biggest issues is the amount of waste that is produced, which can be (2) _______ (CONSUME) quickly. This can lead to pollution and other environmental problems. The use of renewable energy sources can help to (3) _______ (MITIGATE) these problems, but it is not always a viable option.

questions.question: IMPROVE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'improvement' because it refers to the process of making something better or more effective. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a word formation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: CONSUME
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'consumption' because it refers to the act of using or spending something. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a word formation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: MITIGATE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'mitigation' because it refers to the act of reducing or minimizing something. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a word formation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the word that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 4: Key Word Transformations on Educational Philosophy
parts.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given.

questions.question: I haven't seen her for ages. SINCE: It's been a long time _______ her.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'since I last saw her' because it refers to the amount of time that has passed since the speaker last saw the person. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a key word transformation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the word appears. Then, choose the phrase that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: The new policy has been widely criticized. AS A RESULT: _______ have been many protests against it.
questions.correctOption: as a result there have been
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'as a result there have been' because it refers to the consequence or outcome of the policy. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a key word transformation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the word appears. Then, choose the phrase that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: The company is experiencing financial difficulties. DESPITE: _______ they are still planning to expand their operations.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'despite the fact that' because it refers to the contrary or opposite of what is expected. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a key word transformation task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the word appears. Then, choose the phrase that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 5: Multiple-Choice Reading Comprehension on Educational Research
parts.instructions: Read the text and answer Questions 1-6. For each question, choose the correct answer A, B, C or D.
parts.content: Educational research has shown that students who are given more autonomy in their learning tend to perform better than those who are not. This is because autonomy allows students to take an active role in their own learning, which can lead to greater engagement and motivation. However, some researchers have argued that giving students too much autonomy can lead to a lack of structure and organization in their learning. This can result in students becoming overwhelmed and struggling to stay on top of their work. On the other hand, some studies have shown that students who are given more autonomy tend to have better social and emotional skills, such as communication and problem-solving. Overall, the evidence suggests that giving students more autonomy can have both positive and negative effects, depending on the context and the individual student.

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about the effect of autonomy on student performance?
options: A. It has a negative effect
B. It has a positive effect
C. It has no effect
D. It depends on the context
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'it has a positive effect' because the writer suggests that students who are given more autonomy tend to perform better than those who are not. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice reading comprehension task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the question appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: What is one of the potential drawbacks of giving students too much autonomy?
options: A. Lack of structure and organization
B. Overwhelm and struggling to stay on top of work
C. Poor social and emotional skills
D. Lack of motivation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'lack of structure and organization' because the writer suggests that giving students too much autonomy can lead to a lack of structure and organization in their learning. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice reading comprehension task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the question appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: What do some researchers argue about the effect of giving students autonomy?
options: A. That it has a positive effect
B. That it has a negative effect
C. That it depends on the context
D. That it has no effect
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'that it has a negative effect' because some researchers have argued that giving students too much autonomy can lead to a lack of structure and organization in their learning. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-choice reading comprehension task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the question appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 6: Gapped Text on Educational Policy
parts.instructions: Six sentences have been removed from the text. Choose from the sentences A-G the one which fits each gap. There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.
parts.content: The new educational policy has been widely criticized by teachers and parents. [Gap 1] They argue that the policy will lead to a lack of resources for schools. [Gap 2] This will have a negative impact on student learning and achievement. [Gap 3] Some experts have suggested that the policy is too rigid and does not take into account the needs of individual students. [Gap 4] The policy has also been criticized for its lack of transparency and accountability. [Gap 5] The government has promised to review the policy and make changes as necessary. [Gap 6] In the meantime, schools will continue to implement the policy as planned. The policy will be evaluated in six months' time.

questions.question: Gap 1
options: A. However, the government argues that the policy will have many benefits for students and teachers.
B. The policy has been designed to improve student outcomes and increase teacher satisfaction.
C. The new educational policy has been widely criticized by teachers and parents.
D. The policy will also lead to a lack of resources for schools and a negative impact on student learning and achievement.
E. The policy has been implemented to improve student learning and teacher satisfaction.
F. Some experts have suggested that the policy is too rigid and does not take into account the needs of individual students.
G. The policy has also been criticized for its lack of transparency and accountability.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'However, the government argues that the policy will have many benefits for students and teachers' because it provides a contrasting view to the criticism of the policy. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a gapped text task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

C. The new educational policy has been widely criticized by teachers and parents.
D. Some experts have suggested that the policy is too rigid and does not take into account the needs of individual students.
E. The policy will also lead to a lack of resources for schools and a negative impact on student learning and achievement.
F. The policy has been implemented to improve student learning and teacher satisfaction.
G. The policy has also been criticized for its lack of transparency and accountability.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'The policy will also lead to a lack of resources for schools and a negative impact on student learning and achievement' because it provides a consequence of the policy. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a gapped text task, read the text carefully and identify the context in which the gap appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.

parts.partTitle: Part 7: Multiple Matching on Educational Research
parts.instructions: Read the texts below. For Questions 1-6, choose from the sections A-D. Each section may be chosen more than once.

content.A: Educational research has shown that students who are given more autonomy in their learning tend to perform better than those who are not. This is because autonomy allows students to take an active role in their own learning, which can lead to greater engagement and motivation.
content.B: However, some researchers have argued that giving students too much autonomy can lead to a lack of structure and organization in their learning. This can result in students becoming overwhelmed and struggling to stay on top of their work.
content.C: On the other hand, some studies have shown that students who are given more autonomy tend to have better social and emotional skills, such as communication and problem-solving.
content.D: Overall, the evidence suggests that giving students more autonomy can have both positive and negative effects, depending on the context and the individual student.

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about the effect of autonomy on student performance?
options: A
B
C
D
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A' because the writer suggests that students who are given more autonomy tend to perform better than those who are not. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: When completing a multiple-matching task, read the texts carefully and identify the context in which the question appears. Then, choose the option that best fits the meaning of the sentence.
